Rhus-t..personality: The Rhus Toxicodendron constitutional type is fundamentally restless and driven, unable to remain still yet quickly exhausted by sustained effort. They are industrious people who must keep moving to feel comfortable, but their energy is limited and they cycle between activity and collapse. There is a deep physical and mental rigidity that loosens with warming up, mirroring their stiff, guarded approach to life.
Rhus-t..mental_state: Mentally, the Rhus type suffers from prostration and inability to sustain mental effort, with a mind that seizes up like their joints and only functions after warming into a task. There is incoherent thinking under stress, anxiety with apprehension especially at night, and a pervasive sense of impending misfortune. Despondency and disgust for life dominate the chronic state, with suicidal ideation yet lacking the courage to act on it.
Rhus-t..emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern is one of profound sadness and weeping without known cause, coupled with intense irritability and a feeling that some misfortune has occurred. They experience extreme anxiety and nervous restlessness in both acute and chronic states, with fears that intensify markedly at night. There is an ambivalence at their center — they desire death yet fear it, want relief yet cannot rest — reflecting an emotional life trapped between despair and agitation.
